acceptance_criteria: A glyph manifest fixture resolves every theorem id and dictionary id.
Unproved theorem ids render as planned/exploratory/blocked rather than proved.
A Living Book page links the glyph fixture to the source manifest and Lean declarations.
targets.edge_question: Can a visual glyph resolve to theorem ids, Lean declarations, dictionary terms, and proof status without prose drift?
targets.guardrail: A diagram is an interface, not a proof.
targets.next_action: Generated glyph fixture now resolves proved and unproved theorem statuses; expand toward glyph syntax trees and normal forms.
targets.ordinary_baseline: Static mathematical diagrams plus separate theorem statements.
targets.title: Glyph-to-theorem status compiler

acceptance_criteria: A Python fixture normalizes finite-circle examples to gcd, period, orbit count, and full-coil signatures.
At least ten existing theorem ids are indexed by those signatures.
A comparison notes which lookups are easier by signature and which are not.
targets.edge_question: Can finite-coil signatures expose useful theorem candidates or reduce search friction in bounded S1 modular domains?
targets.guardrail: Do not claim global theorem discovery from a bounded-domain index.
targets.next_action: Bounded S1 theorem-signature index added; expand toward generated theorem metadata and compare against ordinary name/type lookup.
targets.ordinary_baseline: Name/type search over theorem declarations and ordinary modular-arithmetic lemmas.
targets.title: Finite-coil normal-form theorem search

acceptance_criteria: A provenance object records at least divisor, cofactor, orbit-count, period, and theorem-link views for selected n.
The fixture compares value-only lookup with provenance lookup on a small set of explanations.
The paper text identifies where provenance adds clarity and where it adds noise.
targets.edge_question: Can construction histories for a number compress or clarify divisor, factor, orbit, and proof navigation better than value-only presentation?
targets.guardrail: Standard mathematics already represents these facts; the claim is interface leverage only.
targets.next_action: Minimal NumberProvenance fixture added for small finite circles; expand only when it improves explanations without adding noise.
targets.ordinary_baseline: A numeric value plus separate factorization and theorem links.
targets.title: Number provenance compression fixture

acceptance_criteria: Noisy, aliased, and multi-period synthetic fixtures are deterministic.
Coil closure, autocorrelation, and periodogram-style baselines are all reported.
Negative or ambiguous results are recorded without upgrading claims.
targets.edge_question: Can coil closure profiles or prime-lag scans add useful diagnostics beyond autocorrelation and periodogram baselines?
targets.guardrail: Benchmark results are not Lean proofs and do not imply real-data usefulness.
targets.next_action: APPD-B0002 adds deterministic noisy, aliased, multi-period, and periodogram-style baseline cases; next step is external periodogram/Fourier baselines and real-data guardrails.
targets.ordinary_baseline: Autocorrelation, periodogram/Fourier ranking, and known-period synthetic controls.
targets.title: Noisy and aliased coil-signature benchmark

acceptance_criteria: The benchmark includes positive periodic tasks and negative nonperiodic controls.
CPU and optional MLX paths produce matching deterministic scores on the tiny fixture.
The result report separates quality, runtime, parameter count, and interpretability.
targets.edge_question: Can explicit phase channels help only on tasks with real periodic structure, compared against ordinary learned baselines?
targets.guardrail: No claim that circles improve all AI.
targets.next_action: AIA-B0002 adds periodic and nonperiodic learned-baseline controls; AIA-B0004 adds dense-scalar, learned-position, wrong-period, cyclic-feature, and nonperiodic controls; AIA-B0005 adds harmonic/Fourier correct-frequency and wrong-frequency controls; expand next to standard RoPE, dense learned, and MLX model baselines.
targets.ordinary_baseline: Dense learned features, standard positional features, RoPE-style encodings, and nonperiodic controls.
targets.title: Learned phase-channel baseline comparison

acceptance_criteria: The CoilLayout validation grid expands to at least one workload-specific stencil or sampling fixture.
Direct/dense baselines and circular-layout variants agree on expected outputs.
Runtime observations are recorded as measurements, not proof claims.
targets.edge_question: Can circular schedules improve locality, explainability, or verification for workloads that are already periodic or angular?
targets.guardrail: Performance claims require direct baselines and repeated measurements.
targets.next_action: COMPL-B0002 adds a periodic-boundary stencil fixture; expand next toward angular sampling and repeated direct/dense/FFT/NTT/MLX backend measurements.
targets.ordinary_baseline: Direct dense traversal, ordinary stencil loops, FFT/NTT baselines, and standard angular sampling.
targets.title: Circular layout and sampling backend comparison

acceptance_criteria: The note gives a bounded orientation-debugging workflow tied to existing S3 theorem ids.
It distinguishes quaternion algebra facts from SO(3) quotient/topology claims.
At least one Python example shows q and -q sign ambiguity without claiming full robotics verification.
targets.edge_question: Can quaternion/Hopf/fiber language make orientation ambiguity, phase, and spin double-cover behavior easier to verify or debug?
targets.guardrail: Do not claim a complete SO(3), robotics, gauge, or physics formalization.
targets.next_action: Hopf hidden-phase and spin sign widgets are in place with Python parity checks, and the spin lane now has Lean-proved pure-imaginary preservation for the bounded vector input convention; future work needs norm/action-composition proofs plus a precise SO(3) quotient/vector-space model before stronger robotics claims and a scoped physics model before Berry/geometric-phase claims.
targets.ordinary_baseline: Quaternion formulas, SO(3) conventions, and separate visualization tools.
targets.title: Quaternion and Hopf orientation-debugging note

acceptance_criteria: A reader path links S0, S1, S2, S3, applications, theorem status, dictionary entries, and source files.
Every theorem badge resolves from generated data.
Every interactive example is labeled as intuition/executable reference rather than proof.
targets.edge_question: Can the Living Book make cyclic and dimensional ideas easier to learn while preserving formal proof status?
targets.guardrail: Do not weaken theorem status language for pedagogical smoothness.
targets.next_action: Reader path page added; next evaluate whether the path needs page-level screenshots or reader-task checks after more widgets mature.
targets.ordinary_baseline: A static paper sequence with separate code and proof files.
targets.title: Living Book learning-path evaluation

acceptance_criteria: The AI roadmap separates proof targets, benchmark fixtures, model prototypes, and speculative research hypotheses.
Every positive fixture includes at least one negative or nonperiodic control.
Benchmarks compare against ordinary AI baselines and report quality, runtime, memory, parameter count, and interpretability when relevant.
No AI improvement claim is promoted unless the exact workload, baseline, metric, and reproducible script are present.
targets.edge_question: Across phase features, cyclic memory, sparse/coil attention, adapter blocks, RoPE variants, recurrent state, harmonic features, and geometry-aware models, where does explicit circle structure actually help compared with ordinary AI baselines?
targets.guardrail: AI importance does not relax proof status or benchmark discipline: finite index proofs are not model-quality claims.
targets.next_action: AIA-B0003 adds CPU/optional-MLX backend parity scaffolding, AIA-B0004 adds learned-feature baseline controls, AIA-B0005 adds harmonic/Fourier-feature baseline controls, AIM-B0001 adds a deterministic cyclic-memory fixture, AIM-B0002 adds a deterministic coil-retrieval reachability fixture, AIM-B0003 adds a deterministic looped-recurrence schedule fixture, AIM-B0011 adds a deterministic loop-exit certificate fixture, AIM-B0004 adds a deterministic content-gated retrieval fixture, AIM-B0005 adds a deterministic token-level recurrence routing fixture, AIM-B0012 adds a deterministic learned token-level recurrence fixture, AIM-B0006 adds a deterministic training-free loop-wrapper fixture, AIM-B0007 adds a deterministic middle-block recurrence fixture, AIM-B0013 adds a deterministic learned middle-block recurrence fixture, AIM-B0008 adds a deterministic multi-resolution recurrence fixture, AIM-B0014 adds a deterministic learned multi-resolution recurrence fixture, AIM-B0009 adds a deterministic learned recurrence-schedule fixture, AIM-B0010 adds a deterministic learned content-gate retrieval fixture, AIRA-B0001 adds a deterministic adapter-block fixture, AIRA-B0002 adds a deterministic MultiCoil/RoPE-style positional fixture, AIRA-B0003 adds a deterministic RoPE-style relative phase fixture, AIRA-B0004 adds a deterministic adapter parameter-budget fixture, and AIRA-B0005 adds a deterministic circulant-mixer validation fixture, each with ordinary baselines and a negative or control case where applicable; expand next toward denser learned/content-gated coil attention, denser learned recurrence-schedule fixtures, dense/LoRA/block-circulant/circulant baselines, standard RoPE, Universal Transformer, looped-transformer, recurrent-memory, sparse/MoE, RWKV/Mamba-style, actual MLX model prototypes, and timing only after parity is stable.
targets.ordinary_baseline: Dense learned features, standard attention, sliding-window and sparse attention, RoPE and learned positional encodings, LoRA and dense adapters, state-space and convolutional sequence models, Fourier features, and ordinary periodic feature engineering.
targets.title: Full-spectrum Circle AI application program

acceptance_criteria: A recurrence-schedule fixture records loop phase, budget, exit step, score trace, and overthinking boundary on a deterministic task.
The fixture compares fixed-depth, fixed-loop, adaptive-exit, over-looped, recurrent-memory, and dense baseline controls where practical.
Learned token-level fixtures explicitly record loop budgets, active-token sets, wrong-period controls, shifted-budget controls, and over-loop controls where relevant.

Learned middle-block fixtures explicitly record phase-to-block and phase-to-budget lookup tables, selected-band/full-block baselines, wrong-period controls, wrong-block controls, and over-loop controls where relevant.

Learned multi-resolution fixtures explicitly record phase-to-budget and phase-to-resolution lookup tables, single-resolution/fixed-budget baselines, wrong-period controls, and over-loop controls where relevant.
Any Lean theorem target is restricted to finite schedule/index/closure facts, not model accuracy or reasoning quality.
The Living Book AI chapter teaches looped recurrence as an auditable schedule with limitations before showing source links.
targets.edge_question: Can Circle Calculus make looped/recursive transformer compute depth more auditable by tracking loop phase, closure/exit certificates, recurrence provenance, and overthinking boundaries?
targets.guardrail: Do not claim looped Circle AI improves reasoning, quality, context length, memory, speed, or parameter efficiency until a reproducible benchmark beats ordinary baselines on a named workload.
targets.next_action: AIM-B0003 now adds an exploratory Python recurrence-schedule benchmark fixture with single-pass, fixed-loop, adaptive-exit, recurrent-memory, sparse phase-router, over-looped, and dense-threshold controls; AIM-B0011 adds a loop-exit certificate fixture with score trace, exit availability, budget status, and overthinking guardrail status; AIM-B0005 adds token-level recurrence routing with per-token budgets, active-token counts, selected middle block, resolution labels, fixed/wrong/over-loop controls, and a nonperiodic scalar-threshold control; AIM-B0012 adds learned token-level recurrence routing with a phase-to-budget lookup, fixed-budget baseline, wrong-period control, shifted-budget control, over-loop control, and nonperiodic scalar-threshold control; AIM-B0006 adds training-free loop-wrapper controls; AIM-B0007 adds middle-block recurrence controls with selected-block, full-block, fixed-budget, wrong-block, and over-loop baselines; AIM-B0013 adds learned middle-block recurrence controls with phase-to-block and phase-to-budget lookup tables, selected-band/full-block baselines, fixed-budget, wrong-period, wrong-block, and over-loop controls; AIM-B0008 adds multi-resolution recurrence controls with single-resolution, fixed-budget, wrong-resolution, and over-loop baselines; AIM-B0014 adds learned multi-resolution recurrence controls with phase-to-budget and phase-to-resolution lookup tables plus single-resolution, fixed-budget, wrong-period, and over-loop controls; AIM-B0009 adds a learned recurrence-schedule fixture with fixed-budget, wrong-period, and over-loop controls; expand next toward learned recurrence fixtures with Universal Transformer, fixed looped-transformer, recurrent-memory, token-level Mixture-of-Recursions, sparse/MoE, RWKV/Mamba-style, and state-space baseline implementations, then add finite Lean schedule facts only if they extend beyond existing phase-channel closure theorems.
targets.ordinary_baseline: Dense transformer depth, Universal Transformer recurrence, fixed looped transformers, adaptive early-exit models, recurrent-memory transformers, token-level Mixture-of-Recursions, middle-block recurrence, multi-resolution recurrence, training-free loop wrappers, sparse/MoE looped models, RWKV/Mamba-style recurrent/state-space models, and standard nonrecursive transformer baselines.
targets.title: Phase-indexed looped transformer recurrence

acceptance_criteria: A Circle-side transfer note states the exact recurrence, routing, memory, phase-feature, mixer, and seed-rule contracts without copying private Theseus-Hive report data into the public Circle repo.

A local report-only Theseus-Hive consumer loads the Circle contract pack, compares every contract family against ordinary baseline categories, and emits separate quality, runtime, memory, parameter, interpretability, transfer, and failure-case axes without treating the result as promotion evidence.
A local smoke workload layer gives all six contract families stable named structural workload slots while keeping model-quality metrics absent until real private benchmarks exist.
A local deterministic proxy benchmark layer measures baseline metrics for all six contract families while explicitly marking learned-model quality metrics and real private workload results absent.

A local aggregate attachment layer reads private/synthetic/report-summary Theseus-Hive artifacts, emits aggregate-only workload ids for all six families, and explicitly marks learned-model quality metrics, real private model benchmark results, training mutation, promotion evidence, external inference, and private data export absent.
Each proposed Theseus-Hive experiment has ordinary baselines, negative controls where applicable, and separate quality, runtime, memory, parameter, and interpretability metrics.
Lean-backed claims are restricted to finite schedule/index/coverage/equivariance facts such as AIM/AIT/AIRA theorem ids; model-quality claims remain benchmark-only.
The Living Book AI chapter and application paper explain how Theseus-Hive can consume Circle contracts without treating fixtures as proofs or public-promotion evidence.
targets.edge_question: Can Circle Calculus make Theseus-Hive recurrence schedules, candidate fanout, context memory, phase features, circulant mixers, and seed-rule generators more auditable and experimentally useful?
targets.guardrail: Do not claim Circle Calculus has improved Theseus-Hive, LLM reasoning, context length, throughput, or public transfer until a named private or public-safe workload beats ordinary baselines with reproducible evidence.
targets.next_action: Public Circle-side JSON contract exporter added, and local report-only Theseus-Hive consumers now load all six contract families, emit YELLOW private-planning, structural-smoke, deterministic-proxy, and aggregate real-workload attachment reports with zero external inference, no training mutation, no promotion evidence, 23 ordinary-baseline comparison rows, six named smoke workloads, six proxy benchmark ids, and six aggregate attachment ids: circle_recurrence_budget_real_rows_v1, circle_candidate_fanout_real_manifest_v1, circle_memory_alias_real_trace_v1, circle_phase_feature_real_sequence_v1, circle_mixer_real_report_summary_v1, and circle_seed_rule_real_provenance_summary_v1. Next promote those aggregate attachments into scored private benchmarks before making usefulness claims.
targets.ordinary_baseline: Theseus-Hive's existing position buckets, sequential or stratified work-budget admission, ordinary candidate fanout, score-based context retention, dense or low-rank mixers, and object-only artifact storage.
targets.title: Proof-carrying Circle contracts for Theseus-Hive
